purpose: Single metadata surface for SDK, CLI, TypeScript MCP, Go MCP, docs, and gates.

sourcePolicy.generatedEditRule: Do not hand-edit spec/corrected, output/ts-sdk, or wrapper/src.

sdk.prepublishOnly: npm run sync && npm run type-check && npm test && npm run clean && npm run build && npm run build:smoke

cli.prepublishOnly: npm run type-check && npm test && npm run build

tsMcp.prepublishOnly: npm run type-check && npm test && npm run build

workflows.userGoal: Confirm authentication, workspace, user, and running timer state.

surfaceAvailability.sdk: supported through diagnostics, health, workspace, and time-entry APIs
surfaceAvailability.cli: supported through status and doctor commands
surfaceAvailability.tsMcp: supported through status tool and doctor resource

workflows.proofMode: mock/unit plus live sandbox status probes when credentials are present
recovery: Use diagnostics request IDs and stable error codes before changing auth or workspace configuration.

workflows.userGoal: Diagnose first-run setup issues and prepare a safe support handoff before mock or live proof.

surfaceAvailability.sdk: supported through no-network diagnostics and generated support bundle metadata
surfaceAvailability.cli: supported through doctor/status plus quickstart and support-bundle gates
surfaceAvailability.tsMcp: supported through the doctor resource, not a mutating tool
surfaceAvailability.goMcp: supported through the matching doctor resource

workflows.proofMode: no-network diagnostics and support-bundle contracts before mock or live sandbox proof
recovery: Use readinessContext, safeCommandHints, and diagnostic receipt output before asking for logs or retrying live calls.
intentionalGaps: This workflow is diagnostics/support-only; it must not mutate Clockify, publish packages, or use customer workspaces.

workflows.userGoal: Start, stop, switch, log, list, update, and delete time entries.

surfaceAvailability.sdk: supported through time-entry resources and pagination helpers
surfaceAvailability.cli: supported through first-class start, stop, log, list, and delete commands
surfaceAvailability.tsMcp: supported through workflow tools for start, stop, switch, log, review, and fix

workflows.proofMode: unit plus live sandbox create/update/delete flows when credentials are present
recovery: Prefer returned entry IDs; after ambiguous writes, re-list by ID or bounded date range before retrying.

workflows.userGoal: Create or reuse clients, projects, tasks, and tags before logging work.

surfaceAvailability.sdk: supported through client, project, task, and tag resources
surfaceAvailability.cli: supported through list/create commands for clients, projects, tasks, and tags
surfaceAvailability.tsMcp: supported through create-work-package workflow plus domain tools
surfaceAvailability.goMcp: supported through matching workflow and domain tools

workflows.proofMode: resource docs, CLI tests, and MCP workflow tests
recovery: Reuse existing objects when receipts report reuse; clean up by explicit IDs or timestamped prefixes.

workflows.userGoal: Handle invoices, expenses, time off, scheduling, webhooks, and audit logs safely.

surfaceAvailability.sdk: supported through generated resources plus wrapper recovery helpers
surfaceAvailability.cli: supported through list/create/search commands where the CLI exposes the workflow
surfaceAvailability.tsMcp: supported through dry-run and confirmation-token workflow tools
surfaceAvailability.goMcp: supported through matching workflow tools and raw fallback where documented

workflows.proofMode: contract evidence, sandbox MCP flows, and GOCLMCP parity docs
recovery: Use dry_run and confirm_token before high-risk writes; preserve request IDs and discrepancy anchors.

workflows.userGoal: Seed deterministic demo objects and remove them by prefix after tests or demos.

surfaceAvailability.sdk: supported through underlying resources used by the demo workflow
surfaceAvailability.cli: no dedicated demo workflow command; use normal create/list/delete commands in a sacrificial workspace
surfaceAvailability.tsMcp: supported through dedicated demo seed and cleanup tools
surfaceAvailability.goMcp: supported through matching demo seed and cleanup tools

workflows.proofMode: aggregate live orchestrator plus dependency-ordered cleanup and zero-leftover receipt
recovery: Use identifiable prefixes and cleanup receipts; never run demo cleanup against a customer workspace.
intentionalGaps: CLI has no dedicated demo seed/cleanup command; this is intentionally an MCP-first workflow.
